Helping you sell
your home
for more.

Global brands invest millions in how they present their products to the world. Selling your home deserves the same thinking - and more.

Book a free consultation

Your home is an ad.

If your home is going on the market, it's a product competing for attention in a crowded field.

Yet most sellers approach the sale with a quick tidy, a few finishing touches, and hope for the best.

We apply the same strategic thinking used by the world's leading brands - to help you achieve the best possible price from the biggest sale of your life.

Behind Every Door works with home sellers to improve property presentation, buyer perception and market readiness before a property goes live. Through the Viewer Lens, we identify the details that influence how buyers experience a home and ultimately what they're willing to pay for it.

What we do

This isn't interior design.

Interior design is for the people living in a home.

Home staging is for the seller - so that potential buyers can imagine their lives there.

There's a difference - and it changes everything.

We think about your home in four ways. How it's:

  • ◇Positioned
  • ◇Prepared
  • ◇Presented
  • ◇Brought to market
How we do it

The Viewer Lens.

We apply our Viewer Lens - a way of seeing your home exactly as a buyer does.

Most buyers can't articulate what puts them off a property. They just know something doesn't feel right. They stay less time. They make a lower offer. Or they don't make one at all.

These are experience fractures - the moments where buyer confidence quietly breaks. They're where value is lost.

The Viewer Lens finds them. Before a buyer does.

Kerb appeal and first impression

What a buyer feels before they've crossed the threshold. The front door, the approach, the immediate sense of whether this feels like somewhere worth their time.

The arrival moment

The hallway or entrance. The first seven seconds inside. The emotional tone it sets for everything that follows.

Room by room

How each space reads, connects to the next, and contributes to an overall impression.

The photography brief and listing copy

How the home should be captured and described - where 90% of buyer decisions now begin.

Our process

Three steps to market-ready.

01

Viewer Lens Audit

We assess your home exactly as a buyer would - from the moment they pull up outside to the final room they walk through. Identifying where interest drops, confidence dips, or the space isn't telling the right story.

02

Spruce. Stage. Sell. Proposal

You receive a clear, prioritised plan tailored to your home.

  • ◇ Spruce - declutter, clean, repair
  • ◇ Stage - presentation, layout, finishing touches
  • ◇ Sell - photography brief and listing copy
03

Take it forward

Implement the changes yourself using our guidance - or work with us to bring everything together.

A note on investment.

Preparation costs money. Not preparing costs more.

The first price reduction on an unsold home is typically 5-20 times greater than the cost of getting it right from the start.

The question isn't whether you can afford to invest in preparation.
It's whether you can afford not to.
